payload.title: README rewrite for first-time visitors
payload.description:
The current README is what bri started with — written for a private dev workspace. Rewrite for a cold first-time visitor (developer or agent landing from HN / a search result / a coworker).

Structure:
1. **Headline** (3 lines max): what Trust is, what it gives you, the eval number. 'Strict Rust dialect for LLMs. 60% of agent code has bugs you can grep for. With #![strict] only 0% ship.'
2. **One install command + one code sample** (before vs after, side-by-side or sequential).
3. **The number** — pull the table from eval/runs/006/summary.md (or a synthesized 3-vendor table). Link the eval directory.
4. **Why** — 2-3 sentences with link to docs/WHY.md.
5. **Status** — '0.1, agent-authored evaluation harness, X case studies, MIT/Apache-2.0.'
6. **Links** — case studies, eval runs, WHY, contributing-not-yet.

Things to AVOID: don't bury the install, don't lead with architecture, don't claim more than the data shows ('100% catch on the audited rules' not '100% bug-free Rust').

Done when: a stranger can read it top-to-bottom in 90 seconds and know whether they want to try it.
